LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-DEFENDANT HIPPWRAP CONTAINMENT, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION. THE FACTS IN THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT SHOW THAT BOTH PARTIES DID NOT SIGN THE SUBCONTRACT AGREEMENT BEFORE THE INJURY TO PLAINTIFF OCCURRED ON NOVEMBER 17, 2011. (FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT, 13, 15.) FOLLOWING NIELSEN CONSTRUCTION COMPANY V. INTERNATIONAL IRON PRODUCTS (1993) 18 CAL.APP.4TH 863, 869, THE EXPRESS INDEMNITY CLAUSE IN THE SUBCONTRACT AGREEMENT IS UNENFORCEABLE UNDER LABOR CODE 3864 BECAUSE BOTH PARTIES DID NOT SIGN THE SUBCONTRACT AGREEMENT BEFORE THE INJURY OCCURRED. THE CITY OF OAKLAND V. DELCON ASSOCIATES (1985) 168 CAL.APP.3D 1126, CASE IS NOT CONTROLLING BECAUSE IT IS FACTUALLY DISTINGUISHABLE. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS CROSS-COMPLAINANT RELIES ON FACTS NOT ALLEGED IN THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. CROSS-COMPLAINANT IS GIVEN 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS, IF IT CAN DO IN GOOD FAITH, REGARDING CROSS-DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO OBTAIN AN ADDITIONAL INSURED ENDORSEMENT IN VIOLATION OF THE SUBCONTRACT AGREEMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-03-2014 Continued To Apr-30-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-07-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. TIM M. AGAJANIAN OF ROPERS MAJESKI LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (213) 312-2010 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-COMPLAINANT;
ATTY. JON S. BRICK OF THE LAW OFFICES OF GRECO TRAFICANTE & BRICK, TEL. NO. (619) 234-3660 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-DEFENDANT HIPPWRAP CONTAINMENT, INC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is Sustained. Cross-Defendant's demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the first and second causes of action. The facts in the First Amended Cross-Complaint show that both parties did not sign the Subcontract Agreement before the injury to Plaintiff occurred on November 17, 2011. (First Amended Cross-Complaint, 13, 15.) Following Nielsen Construction Company v. International Iron Products (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 863, 869, the express indemnity clause in the Subcontract Agreement is unenforceable under Labor Code 3864 because both parties did not sign the Subcontract Agreement before the injury occurred. The City of Oakland v. Delcon Associates (1985) 168 Cal.App.3d 1126, case is not controlling because it is factually distinguishable. As to the third cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as Cross-Complainant relies on facts not alleged in the First Amended Cross-Complaint. Cross-Complainant is given 30 days leave to amend to plead facts, if it can do in good faith, regarding Cross-Defendant's failure to obtain an additional insured endorsement in violation of the Subcontract Agreement. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976
